headline: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen Wants eBay, Says He'll Do 'Whatever' It Takes

(NASDAQ:EBAY), framing the proposed deal as a chance to transform one of the largest e-commerce platforms in the U.S. under an owner-operator model. GME stock is moving. headline: Citi Hikes eBay Price Target to $127: Better Execution Drives Q1 Beat
summary: One firm is doubling down on its bull case for the online marketplace. Citi analyst Ronald Josey raised his price target on eBay (NASDAQ:EBAY) stock to $127 from $114, maintaining a Buy rating. The price target raise reflects what Citi describes as improving operational execution and continued momentum across the company’s targeted growth categories. For ... headline: How Low Can Shopify Stock Go?
summary: Shopify (NASDAQ: SHOP) provides the e-commerce infrastructure for a diverse merchant base, including brands such as Skullcandy, Daily Harvest, and Carrier. Despite this established market position, the stock has faced significant selling pressure recently, declining 38% year-to-date and reaching a 52-week low of $97 in May 2026.

headline: Felix Salmon Says GameStop’s $54 Billion eBay Bid Is Mostly a Stunt. Ryan Cohen Wins Either Way.
summary: Felix Salmon called Ryan Cohen’s bid for eBay largely posturing dressed up as M&A. The math agrees. GameStop (NYSE:GME) carries a market cap of roughly $10.25 billion and is offering $125 per eBay share in a 50% cash, 50% stock structure that would require something like $28 billion in cash against $9 billion on the ... headline: SureDone Launches Mobile App, Bringing Amazon, eBay and Shopify Listing Management Into One Mobile Experience
summary: SureDone, a multichannel e-commerce listing, inventory and order management platform, today announced the launch of SureDone Mobile, a new iOS and Android app that brings product management and multichannel listing workflows for Amazon, eBay and Shopify into one mobile experience.

headline: GameStop’s Rejected eBay Bid Raises Questions On Future Dealmaking And Dilution
summary: GameStop (NYSE:GME) submitted an unsolicited US$56b all stock bid to acquire eBay, which eBay's board rejected as neither credible nor attractive. eBay cited concerns about financing, execution risk, and corporate governance, while GameStop has requested an increase in authorized shares, drawing attention to how any large deal might be funded. High profile investor Michael Burry exited his entire position in GameStop after the bid surfaced, adding to questions about the company’s direction...

raw.headline: GameStop’s Rejected eBay Bid Raises Questions On Future Dealmaking And Dilution

High profile investor Michael Burry exited his entire position in GameStop after the bid surfaced, adding to questions about the company’s direction...

headline: A Hot CPI, Chips in Retreat, and $100 Oil: Tuesday's Market in Four Acts
summary: A single bad data point can rattle a session. Three arriving at once - a hotter-than-expected CPI print, oil punching through $100 a barrel, and a Federal Reserve leadership transition landing mid-week - make for the kind of Tuesday that leaves investors quietly recalibrating.

raw.headline: A Hot CPI, Chips in Retreat, and $100 Oil: Tuesday's Market in Four Acts

headline: EBay rejects GameStop's bid as 'neither credible nor attractive'
summary: STORY: :: eBayEBay on Tuesday rejected a $56 billion takeover bid from the much smaller GameStop.Chairman Paul Pressler said in a statement that the proposal was "neither credible nor attractive."He also pointed to other concerns regarding the bid, including its financing, its impact on eBay's long-term growth and the leadership structure of a potentially combined company.:: eBayPressler added that "eBay's Board is confident the company, under its current management team, is well-positioned to continue to drive sustainable growth."GameStop did not immediately respond to a request for comment.Last week, the videogame retailer's CEO, Ryan Cohen, surprised Wall Street with his bid, which included a $20 billion debt financing commitment from TD Bank.:: eBayBut analysts and investors have doubted whether the half-cash, half-stock bid would succeed.:: eBay websiteCohen, who has built a 5% stake in eBay, has signaled he may be ready to take the offer directly to shareholders.Shares in eBay closed up over 2% on Tuesday, while GameStop fell over 3%.

raw.headline: EBay rejects GameStop's bid as 'neither credible nor attractive'
raw.summary: STORY: :: eBayEBay on Tuesday rejected a $56 billion takeover bid from the much smaller GameStop.Chairman Paul Pressler said in a statement that the proposal was "neither credible nor attractive."He also pointed to other concerns regarding the bid, including its financing, its impact on eBay's long-term growth and the leadership structure of a potentially combined company.:: eBayPressler added that "eBay's Board is confident the company, under its current management team, is well-positioned to continue to drive sustainable growth."GameStop did not immediately respond to a request for comment.Last week, the videogame retailer's CEO, Ryan Cohen, surprised Wall Street with his bid, which included a $20 billion debt financing commitment from TD Bank.:: eBayBut analysts and investors have doubted whether the half-cash, half-stock bid would succeed.:: eBay websiteCohen, who has built a 5% stake in eBay, has signaled he may be ready to take the offer directly to shareholders.Shares in eBay closed up over 2% on Tuesday, while GameStop fell over 3%.
